_README: Two-tier taxonomy. Tier 1 (situation_structures) is the ONLY axis the correct action depends on: it sets the 2-class label (reversible_first vs commit_now). Tier 2 (irreversibility_types, traps, perspectives, domains) are DECOYS -- sampled independently of the structure and balanced within each label so no surface cue predicts the answer; the only way to score is to read the structure, which is the principle. reversible_moves are recorded per dilemma, not balanced. The train/held-out split is NOT baked in here: config.HELDOUT_AXIS picks which decoy axis to hold out (domain / perspective / irreversibility_type) and config.HELDOUT_VALUES reserves values of it for the out-of-distribution eval.

situation_structures.name: one_way_door_with_escape
situation_structures.gloss: A genuine one-way door with a cheap reversible move available: do the reversible thing first (taste, draft, back up, test small).

situation_structures.name: delay_is_the_door
situation_structures.gloss: Waiting is itself the irreversible harm -- delay is the one-way door (the wound bleeds, the data leaks, the chance passes). Act now.

situation_structures.name: false_door
situation_structures.gloss: The step FEELS permanent but is actually recoverable (version history, backups, regrowth, refunds), so the rush-fear is misplaced. Proceed now.

perspectives.name: first_person
perspectives.gloss: The person describes their own situation in the first person ('I'm about to...').

perspectives.name: advising_friend
perspectives.gloss: The person describes a friend's situation and is deciding what to tell them.

perspectives.name: professional_role
perspectives.gloss: The person acts in a professional role (clinician, manager, engineer) facing a decision about someone they are responsible for.

perspectives.name: observer
perspectives.gloss: A neutral third-person account of someone facing the decision.

domains.name: money
domains.blurb: spending, saving, investing, and financial commitments

domains.name: technology
domains.blurb: code, data, devices, and digital systems

domains.name: career
domains.blurb: jobs, hiring, quitting, and workplace moves

domains.name: home_diy
domains.blurb: building, repairing, and renovating around the house

domains.name: creative
domains.blurb: writing, design, art, and creative projects

domains.name: gardening
domains.blurb: planting, pruning, and tending a garden

domains.name: education
domains.blurb: studying, exams, applications, and learning choices
